sectors.name: Healthcare & Medical Practices
sectors.targetBuyer: Practice owners and administrators at independent and group practices
sectors.heroProblem: Billing leakage, staffing costs, and compliance exposure eat margin before you see it.

sectors.name: Legal & Law Firms
sectors.targetBuyer: Managing partners, legal ops directors, and general counsel
sectors.heroProblem: Realization rates, write-offs, and overhead bloat are the silent killers of firm profitability.

sectors.name: Real Estate
sectors.targetBuyer: Investors, brokers, and property management operators
sectors.heroProblem: Deals, teams, and properties need systems — or every transaction starts from scratch.

sectors.name: Professional Services
sectors.targetBuyer: CPA firm owners, agency principals, and independent consultants
sectors.heroProblem: Scope creep, underpricing, and inconsistent delivery keep firm revenue below its ceiling.

sectors.name: Retail & E-Commerce
sectors.targetBuyer: Store owners, buyers, and e-commerce founders
sectors.heroProblem: Margin erosion hides in shrink, markdowns, and vendor terms that were never renegotiated.

sectors.name: Restaurants & Hospitality
sectors.targetBuyer: Restaurant owners, GMs, and hospitality operators
sectors.heroProblem: Prime cost above 65% and turnover above 70% are not industry norms — they're problems with solutions.

sectors.name: Construction & Trades
sectors.targetBuyer: General contractors, project managers, and trades owner-operators
sectors.heroProblem: Job cost overruns and owner dependency are the two reasons trades businesses stay stuck.

sectors.name: Non-Profit & Social Enterprise
sectors.targetBuyer: Executive directors, development directors, and finance staff
sectors.heroProblem: Unfundable financials and governance gaps kill grant applications before they're reviewed.

sectors.name: Financial Services
sectors.targetBuyer: RIA owners, insurance agency principals, and mortgage brokers
sectors.heroProblem: Inconsistent client touchpoints and unclear pipelines drive churn that never shows up in reports.

sectors.name: Education & Training
sectors.targetBuyer: Training business owners, school directors, and course creators
sectors.heroProblem: Course economics, enrollment models, and operations get built on assumptions — until they don't work.

sectors.name: Technology & SaaS
sectors.targetBuyer: SaaS founders, product managers, and startup finance leads
sectors.heroProblem: CAC, LTV, and churn math that's never been modeled is a funding conversation waiting to go wrong.

sectors.name: Manufacturing & Distribution
sectors.targetBuyer: Plant managers, supply chain directors, and COOs
sectors.heroProblem: Downtime, inventory bloat, and supplier dependency hide costs that don't show up until margin review.

sectors.name: Government Contracting
sectors.targetBuyer: GovCon CEOs, BD directors, and contracts managers
sectors.heroProblem: Most organizations lose bids for reasons that have nothing to do with their proposal.
